It means that the foundational models are often trained on local data, reflecting local culture, language nuances, and, most importantly, adhering strictly to domestic data privacy laws. In the Middle East, the push for Sovereign AI is not just a technological trend; it is a matter of national security. Governments in the GCC, including Oman, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE, have realized that exporting their data to Silicon Valley models is functionally equivalent to exporting their sovereign intellectual property.
